programs.name: Statewide Medicaid Managed Care Long-Term Care Program (SMMC-LTC) HCBS Waivers
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services (HCBS) waivers under SMMC-LTC to provide non-medical supports like personal care, respite, meal delivery, and adult companion care, allowing seniors to avoid institutional care. These preserve prior waiver benefits in a managed care model with limited enrollment due to funding.[1][3][6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Florida seniors/disabled individuals aged 65+ meeting nursing home level of care, financial limits (income ≤$2,982/month, assets ≤$2,000), often facing waitlists.[2][3]
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive HCBS up to institutional level costs (e.g., $2,982/month individual), including personal care and respite; waitlist-managed enrollment.[2][3]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Combines Medicaid and Medicare benefits into one program providing comprehensive long-term care, medical services, and extras like dental/eye care primarily in community settings. Available in limited Florida sites for eligible dual-eligible seniors.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Florida seniors 55+ certified for nursing home level care, eligible for both Medicare and Medicaid, living in PACE service areas.[2]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitation covering Medicare/Medicaid services plus extras; value equivalent to full LTC coverage without premiums/copays for eligibles.[2]

programs.name: Medicaid Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs (Qualified Medicare Beneficiary, Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary, Qualifying Individual) help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copays for low-income seniors. Administered via Florida Medicaid to reduce out-of-pocket costs.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Florida Medicare beneficiaries 65+ with low income/assets (varies: QMB up to 100% FPL, SLMB/QI higher with limits).[6]
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Part A/B premiums/cost-sharing ($0 premiums); SLMB: Part B premium (~$185/month); QI: Part B premium; annual value $2,000+ per person.[6]

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly food assistance benefits via EBT card to purchase groceries for low-income seniors. Florida administers federally through Department of Children and Families.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Florida seniors 60+ with income ≤130-165% FPL (higher deductions for elderly), limited assets.[8]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per eligible senior household for food purchases.

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Offers help with energy bills, crisis assistance, and weatherproofing for low-income households including seniors. Florida DOEA coordinates federal funds.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Florida low-income seniors 60+ at or below 150% FPL, priority for elderly/disabled.[8]
programs.benefit_value: $200-1,000+ annually in bill assistance per household, one-time payments.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and efficient appliances to reduce utility costs. Federal program via Florida agencies targeting vulnerable including seniors.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Florida households including seniors 60+ below 200% FPL, priority for elderly.[8]
programs.benefit_value: $3,000-7,000 in free home improvements per household, long-term energy savings.

programs.name: SHINE (Serving Health Insurance Needs of Elders)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare, Medicaid, and related options via trained volunteers. Florida DOEA program through Area Agencies on Aging.[9]
programs.who_its_for: All Florida Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, and families seeking insurance guidance.[9]
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling sessions, helping save thousands on premiums/coverage choices annually.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote independence and health. Provided via Florida Area Agencies on Aging.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound Florida seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often low-income or mobility-limited.[8]
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at low/no cost ($2 suggested donation), nutritional support valued at $1,000+/year.

programs.name: Alzheimer's Disease Initiative (ADI) Respite Services
programs.what_it_does: Provides caregiver respite and support services including short-term relief care for dementia patients. Florida DOEA Medicaid waiver program.[6][8]
programs.who_its_for: Florida caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's/dementia meeting Medicaid waiver eligibility.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Up to 360 hours/year respite/personal care, plus training; value $5,000+ annually.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service jobs with training for low-income seniors to gain employment skills. Federal program administered locally in Florida.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed Florida seniors 55+ with income ≤125% FPL.[8]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/week, ~$7,000/year) plus job placement support.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors (Area Agency Legal Services)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for issues like benefits, housing, abuse, and guardianship via Area Agencies on Aging. Florida DOEA coordinates.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Florida seniors 60+ needing civil legal help.[8]
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations/representation, averting losses of $10,000+ in benefits/housing.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care rights. Statewide Florida DOEA program.[8]
programs.who_its_for: Florida seniors in nursing homes, ALFs, and adult family care homes, plus families.[8]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ complaints, protecting rights and improving care conditions.

programs.name: Home Care for the Elderly (HCE) Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides a basic subsidy to support family-type living arrangements for seniors aged 60 and older within private homes. This state-funded program helps caregivers keep elderly relatives out of institutional care by covering essential living expenses.[3][5]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 60+ living in family-type settings with caregivers, meeting state income and functional needs criteria.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Florida-specific state-funded subsidy unique to FL, not a federal program, focused on preventing nursing home placement through family caregiving support.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Basic monthly subsidy (amount varies by eligibility; exact figures available via application through local Aging and Disability Resource Centers).

programs.name: Community Care for the Elderly (CCE) Program
programs.what_it_does: Delivers community-based services like medical supplies, emergency alerts, and therapeutic care in a continuum to support functionally impaired elders. Aims to enable living in the least restrictive, cost-effective home environment.[3][8]
programs.who_its_for: Functionally impaired elders eligible under state guidelines, typically low-income seniors needing non-institutional support.[3]
programs.whats_unique: State-administered FL program providing coordinated in-home services beyond federal Older Americans Act allotments, tailored to avoid institutionalization.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by service package; includes supplies, alerts, and therapy (funded through state appropriations; contact local provider for estimates).

programs.name: Respite for Elders Living in Everyday Families (RELIEF) Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers in-home respite care to give family caregivers breaks while supporting homebound elderly care. Enhances family units' ability to provide ongoing care without burnout.[3][5]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors with family caregivers, meeting income and need-based eligibility through the FL Department of Elder Affairs.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Florida-exclusive state program specifically for in-home respite, distinct from federal caregiver supports, administered statewide.[3][5]
programs.benefit_value: In-home respite hours (duration based on assessment; state-funded, no fixed dollar amount specified).

programs.name: Elder Options
programs.what_it_does: Manages Medicaid waiver programs enabling long-term care in community settings instead of institutions. Coordinates services like home health to promote independence.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors needing long-term care, subject to asset/income limits and waitlists.[1][2]
programs.whats_unique: Florida's state-specific Medicaid State Plan and waiver framework, including unique options like ADI for Alzheimer's, beyond standard federal Medicaid.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Home/community-based services (value equivalent to institutional care; e.g., respite, home health; waitlisted).
